title: oxalis nipponica subsp. kantoensis
intro: Oxalis nipponica subsp. kantoensis, also known as Japanese wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). The plant is known to possess medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of oxalic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of Oxalis nipponica subsp. kantoensis include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to exhibit antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal remedies, including infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe sore throats and promote digestive health.
